original_abstract: Program evaluation serves as a systematic process for collecting data on the implementation and operational outcomes of organizational policies, providing a vital foundation for collective decision-making. This study employs a literature review methodology, synthesizing insights from academic e-books and journals to establish a framework for educational evaluation. The primary objective is to equip educators with the necessary tools and theoretical grounding to conduct rigorous program assessments within school environments. Our findings highlight that analyzing previous school program evaluations is essential for measuring institutional achievement and identifying areas for growth. By integrating current educational policies as evaluative benchmarks, this research offers a strategic roadmap for teachers and supervisors to assess past performance and refine future pedagogical strategies.
humanized_abstract: Program evaluation involves the systematic collection of data about the implementation and operation of policies and other programmes and is fundamental to all collective activity. The present study employed literature review from educational e-books and journals to develop a framework of educational evaluation that can be used by teachers to evaluate the programmes in schools. The main objective of the study was to empower teachers with the necessary skills and knowledge required to carry out programme assessment in schools efficiently. It established that the evaluation of previous school programmes is crucial in assessing school performance and addressing areas requiring improvement. Moreover, the integration of current educational policies as standards of reference provided teachers and inspectors with an effective tool for evaluating past teaching methods and thereby planning for future improvements in instructional programmes.
